topic.what: Cross-campaign attacker re-identification using passive beacon telemetry and Bayesian networks
topic.how: Constructs 24-dimensional behavioral fingerprints from beacon callbacks and incrementally updates attribution posteriors via temporal-decayed cross-campaign confidence
topic.why_matters: Enables longitudinal attribution across campaigns rather than per-incident analysis; reveals fundamental limits of behavioral fingerprinting for actor discrimination

characteristics.what_it_measures: Correct actor re-identification rate across 96 campaigns
characteristics.evidence: Temporal leave-one-out evaluation on synthetic dataset with 8 actors

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean posterior probability assigned to correct actor
characteristics.evidence: Attribution posteriors averaged across all test campaigns
characteristics.context: Well below τc=0.85 threshold for high-confidence attribution

characteristics.what_itasures: Difference between within-actor and cross-actor similarity
characteristics.vs_baseline: Below required ~0.08 for N=8 actors per Proposition 1
characteristics.evidence: t=8.33, p=1.52×10⁻¹⁶; within-actor S̄w=0.848, cross-actor S̄c=0.802
characteristics.context: Separability ceiling from shared OPSEC practices among sophisticated actors

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean fingerprint similarity between distinct actors
characteristics.evidence: Pairwise inter-actor matrix shows all off-diagonal entries >0.89
characteristics.context: Highest pair: DOUBLEPANDA/VOIDLOTUS at 0.99 (same CN origin, shared tool families)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attribution accuracy stability under adversarial adaptation
characteristics.vs_baseline: No significant trend across evasion schedule
characteristics.evidence: Accuracy varies <4.6% from zero to maximum evasion
characteristics.context: Confirms evasion-invariance: feature separability not evasion is binding constraint

characteristics.what_it_measures: Accuracy vs number of training campaigns per actor
characteristics.vs_baseline: No monotonic improvement despite more training data
characteristics.evidence: Ranging from 12.5% to 30.7% as min_train varies from 6 to 1
characteristics.context: Confirms data quantity cannot compensate for feature separability ceiling

good_for: Longitudinal attribution across multiple campaigns over time
Evaluating feature separability limits of behavioral telemetry
Understanding shared OPSEC practices among sophisticated actors
Assessing evasion robustness of attribution frameworks
not_for: High-confidence actor re-identification (posteriors well below τc=0.85)
Open-set attribution (closed set of N=8 assumed)
requires: Synthetic or real longitudinal beacon telemetry dataset
Closed actor set with prior campaign history

novelty: First formal framework for cross-campaign attacker re-identification using beacon telemetry
Bayesian attribution model with cross-campaign confidence (CCC) and separability gap definitions
Temporal leave-one-out evaluation preventing information leakage
Quantification of fingerprint separability ceiling (ΔS=0.046 vs required ~0.08)
open_problems: Validation on real-world passive hack-back deployments (synthetic data only)
Extension to open-set attribution for previously unseen actors
Incorporation of supplementary signals: victim-sector targeting, enriched temporal patterns, infrastructure re-use graphs
Higher-dimensional fingerprints from richer telemetry (HTTP headers, TLS fingerprints, DNS patterns)
